summary: This is a brief announcement post where Cathy O'Neil shares her new Bloomberg View column about the lack of reliable data on sexual harassment prevalence. She also announces she's quitting Twitter because it was negatively affecting her wellbeing. The post itself contains minimal analysis, serving primarily as a pointer to her external column.
key_points: O'Neil published a Bloomberg View piece arguing we lack sufficient data to understand sexual harassment prevalence
She announced quitting Twitter due to its negative emotional impact
The post highlights the gap between #MeToo visibility and actual statistical understanding
money_quotes: We lack the data needed to know how prevalent it is.
I quit Twitter, at least for now. It just kept bringing me down.
key_insight: Even amid heightened awareness of sexual harassment through movements like #MeToo, we still lack the rigorous data needed to truly understand the scope of the problem.
